---
id: jul24-2
name: [Positive Thing]s Aren't Worth Much — Here's Why
technique: contrarian
platforms: [linkedin]
tags: [execution, ideas, mobile-optimized, hook, consistency]
source_month: "2024-07"
---

## Why It Works

This works because it's mobile optimized (short lines) and uses a good opener ("Great ideas aren't worth much") and a good hook ("Here's why:").

## Template

[Positive thing]'s aren't worth much.
Here's why:

All [thing] require [necessary action or process].
And most people struggle to [difficult action].

A [positive description] [thing or concept] with no [necessary action or process]?
That's not worth anything.

A [mediocre description] [thing or concept] with great [necessary action or process]?
Worth [number]x more.

The greatest [experts in a field] and [professionals] understand this equation.
You can become a great one by [starting small].
[Consistent effort] for [long time period].
Not [short time period].

Start [actioning] today.

## Example

Trendy workouts aren't worth much.
Here's why:

All fitness goals require consistent effort.
And most people struggle to maintain discipline.

A revolutionary exercise plan with no follow-through?
That's not worth anything.

A basic workout routine with great commitment?
Worth 10x more.

The greatest athletes and fitness trainers understand this equation.
You can become a great one by putting one foot in front of the other.
Daily practice for years.
Not weeks.

Start exercising today.
